front: Work: attacks the analytic-synthetic distinction and reductionism; uses 'no bachelor is married' vs. 'no unmarried man is married' to discuss cognitive synonymy
back: Two Dogmas of Empiricism (W. V. O. Quine)

front: Concept: the position that no individual statement can be tested in isolation — all of science is tested together; named for its two formulators, one of whom wrote 'Two Dogmas'
Work: introduced the 'Myth of the Given' — an attack on the foundationalist idea that basic sensory concepts can both provide and justify knowledge; also introduced the 'logical space of reasons'
back: Empiricism and the Philosophy of Mind (Wilfrid Sellars)
work: Empiricism and the Philosophy of Mind (Sellars)

front: Work: Paul Grice and P. F. Strawson wrote a 'Defense' of this concept after it was criticized in a Quine paper that also attacks reductionism
back: the analytic-synthetic distinction (defended in response to 'Two Dogmas of Empiricism')

front: Concept: an anti-realist position holding that scientific theories only strive for 'empirical adequacy' rather than truth; developed by Bas van Fraassen
